metric_value: Implementing SAP R/3 throughout an enterprise is a once-in-twenty-year project transforming operational infrastructure
notes: Aberdeen's characterization of R/3 as generational transformation project

metric_value: SAP customers tell Aberdeen R/3 has more functionality than any other client-server integrated business process application
notes: Customer validation of R/3 as functionality leader in ERP category

metric_value: Vast majority of senior executives evaluating or implementing R/3 recognize that use of outside professional services firms is mandatory
notes: Aberdeen field research finding on consultant dependency for R/3

metric_name: Enterprise Introspection Factor 1: Need and Desire for Change
metric_value: Organization must honestly evaluate corporate need and desire for fundamental enterprise change before implementing R/3
notes: First of three mandatory pre-implementation assessments

metric_name: Enterprise Introspection Factor 2: Ability to Adopt Best Practices
metric_value: Company's ability to adopt best practice processes embedded in R/3 must be assessed before implementation
notes: Second of three pre-implementation assessments; R/3 embeds opinionated process designs

metric_name: Enterprise Introspection Factor 3: Internal Resource Availability
metric_value: Availability of internal skills and resources to accomplish both anticipated business and technology changes must be assessed
notes: Third of three pre-implementation assessments; skill gap analysis

metric_value: Departmentally-focused Big Bang implementation: appeal to customers anxious about costs; misses opportunity for enterprise-wide process reengineering
notes: Aberdeen critique of Big Bang approach as missing strategic transformation opportunity

metric_value: Prolonged blue-sky reengineering planning projects proven wasteful; customers frustrated; multi-million technology investment underemployed
notes: Aberdeen critique of over-extended planning with no implementation

metric_value: Consultants can play critical role in raising and framing strategic questions but should not answer them; project team must answer proactively
notes: Key insight distinguishing legitimate consulting from consultant-captured implementations

metric_value: Strategic R/3 change requires: explicit business objective consensus; evaluation of all critical processes; coordination of reengineering with R/3 functionality; aggressive change management
notes: Four-element framework for strategic-level R/3 implementation

metric_value: Aberdeen separately published 'Decision-Makers Guide to Professional Services Support for SAP America Implementations' indicating large consultant-led market
notes: Cross-reference to companion Aberdeen publication on SAP implementation services

metric_value: Enterprise must assess both strategic need for change AND desire for change; executives and managers may differ on both dimensions
notes: Distinguishes organizational politics from technical requirements in implementation

metric_value: Aberdeen implies R/3 will be implemented broadly; professional services for R/3 will be a major industry
notes: Implicit prediction from volume of coverage and recommendation to evaluate

metric_value: SAP R/3/ECC became dominant global ERP platform; SAP consulting became $50B+ annual industry; ASAP methodology institutionalized the introspection process Aberdeen described
notes: Aberdeen's prediction fully validated; SAP and its consultant ecosystem grew dramatically

metric_value: Organization must identify: executive sponsor; core team knowledge workers; change agents in business units
source_page: Availability of Internal Skills and Resources
notes: Human capital assessment component of pre-implementation framework

metric_value: Mismatch of R/3 implementation strategy with sought-after business improvements avoidable via early enterprise introspection
notes: Core thesis of study: pre-implementation analysis prevents costly mismatches

metric_value: Strategic change = enterprise-level wholesale process reengineering; Tactical change = business unit operating efficiency improvement
notes: Key framework dichotomy determining implementation approach and consultant support model

metric_value: End-user project control is critical; questions raised by R/3 implementation must be answered at senior levels quickly and decisively
notes: Governance model that became standard in enterprise IT project management

metric_value: R/3 pioneers report massive reengineering required with strong change management expertise
notes: Based on Aberdeen interviews with early R/3 implementers

metric_value: Aberdeen recommends outside firm with R/3 knowledge AND business grounding to facilitate enterprise introspection before any R/3 project
notes: Predicts consultant-led pre-implementation assessment will become standard

metric_name: Consultant-Led Introspection as Standard Practice - Outcome
metric_value: SAP ASAP methodology (1996), project management frameworks, and pre-implementation readiness assessments became industry standard; Aberdeen's recommendation institutionalized
notes: SAP ASAP methodology incorporated phases directly analogous to Aberdeen's introspection framework
